spec_match.rationale: Demonstrates the six surfaces the spec lists (color, background, border, outline, focus ring, icon fill) but never shows the installed-PWA vs open-web gating — the actual headline shipping behavior.

interactive.rationale: Real color picker that recomputes AccentColorText luminance and repaints six live UI patterns; well past static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Lede promises a live grid of UI patterns updating from a simulated accent — the page delivers exactly six such patterns.

edge_cases.rationale: No demo of non-installed context fallback, no CSS.supports() probe for the keyword itself, no demo of forced-colors / high-contrast interaction which the spec calls out, and the AccentColorText threshold uses luminance>0.179 which is an approximation of the WCAG curve, not the spec's max-contrast rule.

design_system.rationale: All neutral surfaces use CSS variables, focus outline overrides only outline-color (button color stays correct), no static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Add installed-PWA gating probe
openQuestions.detail: The whole reason this keyword shipped in 150 is the installed-PWA gate — open-web pages get a neutral fallback. The page should detect window.matchMedia('(display-mode: standalone)') (or the navigator.windowControlsOverlay path) and show two columns side by side: 'in PWA → resolves to OS accent' vs 'on the open web → neutral fallback'. Without this the demo doesn't actually distinguish 150's behavior from a CSS variable.

openQuestions.title: Use the spec's AccentColorText algorithm
openQuestions.detail: The page computes AccentColorText with a luminance > 0.179 threshold, but the spec algorithm picks the higher contrast ratio between candidate text colors (black or white). The current code can disagree at borderline hues (e.g. mid-orange). Switch to the contrast-ratio comparison the contrast-explorer concept uses, and link to it.

openQuestions.title: Show forced-colors interaction
openQuestions.detail: CSS Color 4 says system colors expose the user-chosen palette in forced-colors mode. Add a CSS @media (forced-colors: active) override that shows what AccentColor resolves to under Windows High Contrast / forced colors, and a button to simulate forced-colors for the demo grid.

openQuestions.title: Probe CSS.supports for AccentColor
openQuestions.detail: Add a small banner at the top that runs CSS.supports('color', 'AccentColor') and shows pass/fail; in non-supporting browsers the demo silently uses the JS picker fallback, which hides the feature-detection story.

summary: Solid surface gallery and well-organised, but the demo simulates AccentColor with a JS-driven CSS variable and never surfaces the actual Chrome 150 behavior change — installed-PWA gating with an open-web fallback. Add a PWA-context probe and forced-colors mode to round it out.
